question: Define a C++ struct named `Book` with members `title` (std::string), `author` (std::string), and `publication_year` (int). Create an an array of three `Book` objects, prompt the user to enter details for each, and then print the details of all books.

test_cases.input:
Title1
Author1
2001
Title2
Author2
2002
Title3
Author3
2003
test_cases.expected:
Please enter details for three books:
Enter details for Book 1:
Enter title: Enter author: Enter publication year: Enter details for Book 2:
Enter title: Enter author: Enter publication year: Enter details for Book 3:
Enter title: Enter author: Enter publication year: 
Book Details:
Title: Title1, Author: Author1, Year: 2001
Title: Title2, Author: Author2, Year: 2002
Title: Title3, Author: Author3, Year: 2003

errors: Array out-of-bounds access: The loop condition `i <= 3` attempts to access `books[3]` which is beyond the allocated size of the `books` array (valid indices 0, 1, 2), leading to undefined behavior.
